name: Reputation Gating
description: A narrative or systemic access rule that unlocks, hides, alters, or blocks content based on faction standing, relationship state, or social trust.
design_notes: Reputation gating turns player history into access rules. Define reputation_faction_id, threshold ids, bypass conditions, lock explanation, save migration, and whether gates are hard blocks or soft alternatives. The player should understand why content is locked without exposing spoilers or reducing faction systems to raw arithmetic.
design_purpose: Make social choices affect available quests, dialogue, vendors, areas, companions, or endings.
Represent faction trust or hostility in world systems that react to player history.
Reward or restrict play styles through accumulated reputation while preserving alternate paths where needed.
player_fantasy: The player feels their history with people and factions has practical consequences in the world.

parameters.name: reputation_faction_id
parameters.description: Faction or relationship track used by the gate.

parameters.name: bypass_condition_ids
parameters.typical_range: item, disguise, quest flag, skill, bribe
parameters.description: Alternate story, item, or skill conditions that bypass the gate.

parameters.name: gate_result_id
parameters.description: Outcome applied when the reputation condition passes or fails.

parameters.name: lock_explanation_policy_id
parameters.description: How the UI explains a locked reputation option.

parameters.name: gate_migration_rule_id
parameters.description: Save migration rule when thresholds move between versions.

edge_cases: Players can belong to hostile factions with conflicting gates and bypass conditions.
Temporary disguises, bribes, or story states may override reputation without changing the underlying standing.
Content patches can move thresholds and affect existing saves in the middle of a quest line.
A hidden gate can look like missing content unless another route or explanation is available.
common_bugs: UI hides a locked option without explaining the reputation requirement or alternate route.
Negative reputation uses the wrong faction key after save migration.
Bypass conditions unlock content but do not set required world_state_flag_id records.
Dialogue display checks one threshold while activation checks another threshold id.
balancing_notes: Hard gates make choices consequential but can hide too much content if no alternate route exists.
Soft gates with bypasses preserve agency while still honoring reputation history.
Locked-option visibility should match the narrative tone: explicit for systems clarity, hidden for mystery, spoiler-safe for story reveals.
accessibility_notes: Explain locked options in plain language and avoid color-only faction standing indicators.
Keep reputation summaries readable in journals or dialogue logs so players can review why a gate changed.

implementation_notes.unity: Store faction standings and gate definitions as data, then evaluate the same condition object for dialogue display, quest activation, vendors, and save migration. UI should receive both gate_result_id and spoiler-safe lock explanation text.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent gates as Resources or dictionaries with reputation_faction_id, threshold id, and bypass conditions, resolved through a central narrative-state service. Save data should store faction ids, not translated names.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Tags, Data Assets, or quest data tables for faction IDs and evaluate reputation conditions in dialogue, vendor, and level-access systems. Replicated sessions should let the host or server own shared gate results.
implementation_notes.web: Keep reputation tracks and gate conditions in JSON, run pure condition checks for both locked-option display and activation, and include bypass reasons in UI data without exposing spoiler-only flags.

relationships.reason: Reputation gates depend on previous choices or state changes that set faction thresholds.
Reputation gates can drive shop access or price modifiers in a readable way.
Reputation-gated abilities need fallback or respec policy if players can lock themselves out permanently.

example_games.title: Fallout: New Vegas
example_games.note: Uses faction standing to change reactions and access to content.

example_games.title: Baldur's Gate 3
example_games.note: Uses relationship and story state to alter dialogue and quest access.
